channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Chain-of-Thought
channel.description: Latest Chain-of-Thought news in Context Engineering

item.title: Disentangling Linguistic Relatedness from Task Alignment in Cross-Lingual Transfer
item.description: arXiv:2606.19346v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study cross-lingual transfer by fine-tuning seven large language models (4B--671B parameters) on Arabic and evaluating zero-shot reading comprehension on Semitic languages and non-Semitic controls. Across dense and Mixture-of-Experts architectures,...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study, chain-of-thought, alignment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: How LLMs Fail and Generalize in RTL Coding for Hardware Design?
item.description: arXiv:2606.19347v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Translating sequential programming priors into the parallel temporal logic of hardware design remains a crucial bottleneck for large language models(LLM). To investigate this, we introduce a new error taxonomy grounded in problem solvability, inspired...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, LLM, alignment, model, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DeepSeek-V4: Towards Highly Efficient Million-Token Context Intelligence
item.description: arXiv:2606.19348v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present a preview version of DeepSeek-V4 series, including two strong Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) language models -- DeepSeek-V4-Pro with 1.6T parameters (49B activated) and DeepSeek-V4-Flash with 284B parameters (13B activated) -- both supporting a c...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, context, model, reasoning, attention | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Pruning via Causal Attribution Preserves Reasoning Performance in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2606.19350v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) excel at multi-step reasoning but incur substantial inference cost. We introduce Causal Attribution Pruning (CAP), a training-free method that identifies critical attention heads by measuring their causal impact on reasoni...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, LLM, model, attention, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.
item.description: [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> chain-of-thought, CoT, reasoning, framework, audio | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: PageIndex - 📑 PageIndex: Document Index for Vectorless, Reasoning-based RAG
item.description: 📑 PageIndex: Document Index for Vectorless, Reasoning-based RAG<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> vector, reasoning, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Cline-Recursive-Chain-of-Thought-System-CRCT- - A framework designed to manage context, dependencies, and tasks in large-scale Cline projects within VS Code
item.description: A framework designed to manage context, dependencies, and tasks in large-scale Cline projects within VS Code<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> chain-of-thought, context, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.
item.description: Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> chain-of-thought, reasoning, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
